hub.subtitle: Eduweer is an educational adventure combining a book, workbook, and app into one cohesive ecosystem.

parent.name: For Parents
parent.desc: Your child learns because they want to — because the story pulls them in. You're right there with them.

investor.name: For Investors
investor.desc: A scalable educational ecosystem — IP, narrative, app, and game in one pipeline.

campaign.name: Support the project
campaign.desc: Be part of the story from the very first chapter. Rewards await.

world.label: One world
world.text: Book, workbook, and app — one cohesive ecosystem

paths.label: Three paths
paths.text: For parents, investors, and campaign supporters

harvoria.label: Harvoria
harvoria.text: The first realm — a calm start to every adventure

meta.title: Eduweer — Learning that starts with adventure
meta.description: Eduweer combines a book, workbook, and Mentor app to turn your child's learning into a calm, shared adventure.

hero.title: Welcome to Eduweer
hero.subtitle: Here your child becomes the hero of a story full of mysteries, challenges and small victories — and every step takes them further.

bridge.question: What if learning felt like <gold>adventure</gold> from the very start?
bridge.lead: A child's first steps in school can be more than tasks to complete — they can become a shared story they always want to come back to.

adventure.title: Learning shouldn't start with a battle
adventure.lead: Harvoria is a world where early school years don't feel like duty. Every lesson is a step further into the story — the book draws you in, the workbook cements it, and the app answers questions your child actually wants to ask.

book.label: Harvoria Book
book.desc: An immersive story where characters learn alongside your child. You read together — and both of you want to know what happens next.

workbook.label: Workbook
workbook.desc: Exercises rooted in the world of Harvoria. No sums for the sake of sums — every task has a story purpose.

app.label: Mentor App
app.desc: A companion from Harvoria who explains, encourages, and motivates. Works offline, no ads, no distractions.

howItWorks.title: Three steps, one rhythm
howItWorks.lead: The Eduweer ecosystem works as a reinforcing loop — each element powers the next and makes your child want to come back on their own.

steps.title: Read together
steps.desc: An evening ritual with the Harvoria book. The story builds context for everything that comes next.

steps.title: Practice in the workbook
steps.desc: Tasks with purpose. Your child solves them to help the characters — not because they have to.

steps.title: Explore in the app
steps.desc: Mentor explains, rewards, and unlocks the next chapters of the world. Progress is visible and felt.

contact.title: Have a question?
contact.lead: We're in the first edition phase. Write to us — we reply personally.

problem.title: Learning shouldn't start with <accent>compulsion</accent>
problem.p1: Many parents know this moment: the child has tasks to do, but quickly loses interest. Instead of curiosity, there's fatigue, negotiation, and tension.
problem.p2: We went through this too, which is why we started looking for a different way. One that doesn't turn learning into pressure, but gives the child a reason to want to keep going. Children engage more easily when they see the purpose. When a task isn't a disconnected sheet, but part of a bigger story.
points: Tasks are embedded in the adventure — the child understands why they're doing them
The parent guides, but doesn't have to fight for every minute of learning
The child sees their hero's progress — the reward is part of the story
Learning becomes part of a shared, calm ritual

what.title: Eduweer is one <accent>adventure</accent> told in several ways

flow.desc: Eduweer was born from the combination of two worlds: calm, valuable reading with your child and the mechanics known from games, which children discover earlier and earlier — a hero, progress, levels, rewards, and the discovery of the next stages.

The difference is that here these mechanics aren't meant to keep the child in front of a screen as long as possible. They're meant to help them see the meaning of effort. Every completed task, every read part of the story, and every appreciated progress means that not only the child develops, but also their hero in the world of Eduweer.

Thanks to this, learning is no longer just an obligation. It becomes part of an adventure in which the child wants to find out what happens next — and the parent can be close, guide the story, and share the child's small victories together.

elements.title: Four elements that <accent>work together</accent>
elements.lead: Eduweer was designed so that children don't switch between random activities. Each element has its role in one adventure.

book.title: The book that opens the world
book.body: The story introduces the child to Eduweer — a land full of adventures, heroes, and challenges. The parent reads it with the child, and each new event naturally leads to tasks and decisions.
bullets: Warm, adventure narrative for children and parents
The child becomes part of the story — not just reads it
Tasks flow naturally from the plot
The parent becomes a guide through the world of Eduweer
book.caption: The story is the beginning of every adventure — it gives meaning to the tasks.

workbook.title: The Hero's Journal where the child truly acts
workbook.body: The workbook is not a disconnected worksheet. It's a place where the child helps heroes, solves puzzles, collects stars, and sees that their effort matters in the world of Eduweer.
bullets: Tasks directly linked to the plot
Space for writing, drawing and solving
Stars awarded by the parent after each task
Sense of agency — the child's work has an effect
workbook.caption: The child doesn't just listen to the story — they help push it forward.

app.title: The Mentor app that helps guide the adventure
app.body: The app doesn't replace the book or the relationship with the child. It's a calm tool for the parent: it shows progress, lets you rate tasks, award stars, and see the hero's development.
bullets: Child and hero profile with level and EXP
Progress map — see where the child is in the adventure
Important actions secured with parent PIN
app.caption: Mentor helps the parent see progress without turning learning into a spreadsheet.

game.title: Eduweer Adventure — the game where the <accent>story lives</accent>
game.body: Eduweer Adventure is a mobile game where the child steps into their hero and completes action and work tasks set in the world of Harvoria. Safe screen settings and the accompanying Mentor character make the game a safe, engaging complement to the entire ecosystem.
bullets: Action and work tasks as extensions of the book adventure
The Mentor character appears in the game — familiar from the app
Safe screen settings designed for young players
In-game progress linked to progress in the book and workbook
game.caption: The game doesn't replace learning — it reinforces it through play in a safe, coherent world.

advHow.title: What does the <accent>adventure</accent> look like step by step?
advHow.lead: Eduweer has a simple rhythm that quickly becomes a natural part of the evening.

steps.title: Read a passage of the story
steps.desc: The parent reads the child the next part of the adventure. The child meets the characters, the setting, and the challenge.

steps.title: The child completes a task
steps.desc: The task in the Hero's Journal flows from the story. The child doesn't solve it 'because they have to', but because they're helping someone in the world of Eduweer.

steps.title: The parent rates the effort
steps.desc: After completing the task, the parent awards 1, 2 or 3 stars. It's a simple way to appreciate the work, focus, and independence.

steps.title: The hero gains progress
steps.desc: In the Mentor app, the child's character develops, gains experience, and advances along the adventure map.

steps.title: The story continues
steps.desc: Next tasks and chapters unlock the continuation of the adventure — the child wants to know what happens next.

child.title: The child sees that learning <accent>matters</accent>
child.lead: The child gets a role in a world that responds to their effort. Every solved task helps them move forward and develop their hero.

cards.title: Curiosity
cards.desc: The story encourages the child to find out what happens next.

cards.title: Agency
cards.desc: Tasks aren't disconnected — they help heroes and unlock next stages.

cards.title: Motivation
cards.desc: Stars, EXP and progress show the child that their work has a visible effect.

cards.title: Development
cards.desc: Thinking, counting, reading, memory and independence — through adventure.

parent.title: The parent doesn't have to be a <accent>teacher</accent>
parent.lead: Eduweer creates a ready-made learning structure at home — calmly, step by step, with a real view of progress.

cards.title: Less fighting over tasks
cards.desc: The child understands why they're doing the task — because it's part of the story.

cards.title: More shared time
cards.desc: Reading and tasks become a family ritual you want to return to.

cards.title: Visible progress
cards.desc: The app shows what has been done and how the child's hero is developing.

cards.title: Simple guidance
cards.desc: The parent doesn't design the process — they simply enter the adventure with the child.

safety.title: Technology should support, not replace, the <accent>relationship</accent>
safety.lead: In Eduweer, the center of the experience remains the shared story, conversation, and tasks done away from the screen. The Mentor app is a tool for the parent — it doesn't take the place of the book or shared time.
points: The app supports the parent, it doesn't replace them
The book and workbook are the physical center of the experience
The child doesn't gain knowledge through mindless clicking
Progress comes only from completed tasks
Important actions are approved by the parent — always in control

badges.title: No ads
badges.desc: No ads targeted at children or intrusive notifications.

badges.title: No pay-to-win
badges.desc: EXP and rewards can only be earned through learning and tasks. Not by buying.

badges.title: Parent PIN
badges.desc: Important actions like task ratings and profile changes require the parent's PIN.

badges.title: Physical center
badges.desc: The book and workbook matter more than the screen — the app is a supporting tool.

day.title: What might one <gold>evening</gold> with Eduweer look like?
day.lead: Eduweer is meant to create a calm rhythm you want to return to. You don't have to do everything at once.

moments.title: You sit together
moments.desc: After dinner you open the book and return to Harvoria. No special place or time needed.

moments.title: You read a passage
moments.desc: The child learns that the heroes need help at the farm. The story gives meaning to what's about to happen.

moments.title: The child solves a task
moments.desc: A task linked to the story appears in the Hero's Journal. The child works independently.

moments.title: The parent awards stars
moments.desc: After the task the parent appreciates the child's effort and marks progress in the Mentor app.

moments.title: The hero moves on
moments.desc: The next step appears on the map. The child sees that their work changed something in the world of Eduweer.

moments.title: You finish calmly
moments.desc: You don't have to do everything at once. Eduweer is meant to create a calm rhythm you want to return to.

items.a: Not only. Eduweer is a combination of a book, workbook, and Mentor app. The app supports the parent, but the heart of the experience remains the story and tasks completed by the child.
The project is designed with children aged around 6–12 in mind. The first volume and first adventure are especially suited to children starting their school education.

items.q: Does the parent need to be a teacher?
items.a: No. The parent takes the role of Mentor — reading, supporting, rating effort and helping the child move forward. The system guides the family step by step, without any pedagogical preparation needed.

items.q: Does the child use a phone?
items.a: The Mentor app is primarily for the parent. The child can see their hero and progress, but the main activity takes place in the book and workbook — not in front of a screen.

Stars are the parent's way of appreciating the child's work. They show completed tasks and translate into rewards in the system, but the most important thing is the sense of visible progress and appreciated effort.

Yes — the game is part of the broader vision of the Eduweer world. At this stage the center of the experience is the book, workbook, and Mentor app. The game will be a future extension of the world that will strengthen engagement and connection to the story.

earlyList.title: Small steps. Big adventures.
earlyList.lead: Eduweer is being created for families who want learning to feel less like pressure and more like a shared journey.
earlyList.body: The first Harvoria workbooks are now in development. By joining the early list, you'll get previews, progress updates, and the chance to be among the first families to try Eduweer before the official launch.
earlyList.success: You're on the list! We'll be in touch with previews and updates.
earlyList.errorEmail: Please enter a valid email address
earlyList.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.

newsletter.title: Join the world of Eduweer
newsletter.lead: We're in the middle of the first Harvoria edition. Sign up for the list — we'll let you know when it's ready and share exclusive previews.
newsletter.success: Thank you! We'll let you know when Harvoria is ready.

contact.title: Write to us
contact.success: Thank you! We'll get back to you soon.
contact.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.

contactForm.lead: If you'd like to learn more about the product, roadmap, investment opportunity, or partnership options, send a message and we'll reply personally.
contactForm.success: Thank you! We'll get back to you personally.

meta.title: Eduweer — Investor Portal · System & Vision
meta.description: A scalable educational ecosystem — IP, narrative, app and game in one pipeline.

hero.title: Eduweer — education as <gold>adventure</gold>, designed as a scalable system.
hero.subtitle: We combine narrative, educational tasks, a parent app and a future adventure game into one closed loop: story → action → assessment → reward → progress → return.
hero.support: We are building a universe where children do not complete tasks "because they have to" — but because every task moves their hero forward in the story and unlocks new elements of the world.

problem.title: Education is losing the fight for a <gold>child's attention.</gold>
problem.subtitle: Children today live in a world of instant stimulation. Traditional learning often gives them no context, emotion or sense of agency.

cards.title: Fast dopamine content
cards.body: Short-form content, games and animations reward faster than learning. Educational tasks often feel slow, disconnected and emotionless by comparison.

cards.title: No context
cards.body: A child often cannot see why they are doing a task. Without story and purpose, learning becomes an obligation rather than a path to discovery.

cards.title: Exhausted parents
cards.body: Parents want to support their child but often lack a tool that guides them calmly through learning and shows real progress.

loop.title: The Closed Educational <gold>Loop</gold>
loop.subtitle: Eduweer turns a single task into part of a bigger adventure. Every step the child takes matters in the story, the app and the hero's development.

steps.title: Story
steps.desc: Narrative gives tasks context. The child knows why they are acting and what is at stake.

steps.title: Task
steps.desc: Exercises build specific school skills, embedded in events from the Eduweer world.

steps.title: Mentor Review
steps.desc: The parent assesses performance and guides the child through the adventure instead of just checking results.

steps.title: Reward
steps.desc: The system grants points, EXP and rewards matched to progress and activity type.

steps.title: Hero Progress
steps.desc: The child sees their character, maps, skills and world elements evolve.

steps.title: Return to World
steps.desc: Progress encourages the child to continue the story and take on new tasks.

why.title: Why does this model work?
points: Story gives purpose to every task
Parent stays at the center of the process
Motivation flows from achievement, not random rewards
System teaches real competencies, not just clicking
Scalable across books, app and games

ecosystem.title: One world, <gold>many entry points.</gold>
ecosystem.subtitle: Eduweer is designed as an ecosystem where physical and digital elements reinforce each other — from the book and workbook to the Mentor app and the upcoming game.

cards.label: Narrative foundation
cards.title: Book + Workbook
cards.body: The book and workbook form the core experience: story, tasks, a rhythm of work and shared time between parent and child. The entry point into the Eduweer world.

cards.label: Parent-guided progress
cards.title: Mentor App
cards.body: The Mentor App lets parents guide their child through the adventure, track progress, rate tasks with stars and watch their hero grow.

cards.label: Long-term engagement
cards.title: Adventure Game
cards.body: The game extends the Eduweer world beyond the book: farming, exploration, crafting and quests that deepen motivation and connection to the IP.

cards.label: Scalable access layer
cards.title: Web / Platform
cards.body: The web portal handles accounts, content, materials, partnerships, schools and future community modules.

flow.title: How a single task becomes part of a bigger <gold>adventure.</gold>
flow.subtitle: Eduweer does not end with solving an exercise. Every task affects progress in the book, the app and the hero.

steps.title: They read
steps.desc: Parent and child read a story passage with a challenge

steps.title: Solves it
steps.desc: The child completes the task in the workbook — because it has narrative meaning

steps.title: Rates it
steps.desc: The parent opens the Mentor App and awards stars

steps.title: Reward
steps.desc: The system calculates EXP and updates the progress map

steps.title: Wants more
steps.desc: The hero grows — the child comes back on their own

traction.title: This is not just a concept. <gold>The foundation already exists.</gold>
traction.subtitle: Eduweer has a built-out world, a drafted book, a defined Mentor app model and a clear direction for the game and platform.

scale.title: Scaling through the world, not just through <gold>features.</gold>
scale.subtitle: Every Eduweer land means a new volume, a new competency set, a new content season and new product opportunities.
scale.dimsSubtitle: Eduweer can grow simultaneously along four independent axes — each opens new markets and revenue streams.

lands.name: Harvoria
lands.body: Farming, responsibility, the rhythm of nature, core school and social competencies.

lands.name: Summitara
lands.body: Mountains, flying ships, trade and conflicts over resource control.

lands.name: Lyra'Glen
lands.body: Giant trees, luminous fungi, ecosystem balance.

lands.name: Mazurai
lands.body: Lakes and rivers, harmony, social relationships, moral choices.

lands.name: Dunnvol
lands.body: Desert, energy, technology and resource responsibility.

lands.name: Futurion
lands.body: The centre of the world, technology and the final conflict.

dims.title: Content scale
dims.body: More books, chapters, quests and tasks for every land.

dims.title: Product scale
dims.body: App, game, portal, board game, educational workshops.

dims.title: Partnership scale
dims.body: Families, schools, institutions, publishers, educational partnerships.

tech.title: Built for progress, data and <gold>expansion.</gold>
tech.subtitle: Eduweer is designed from the start to support progress sync, dynamic rewards, scalable content and future learning personalisation.

biz.title: Revenue without breaking <gold>trust.</gold>
biz.subtitle: Eduweer monetises IP, educational products and digital add-ons without selling a child's progress, EXP or educational advantage.

streams.title: Books & Workbooks
streams.body: Physical and digital editions of books, hero journals and further volumes in the series.

streams.title: Mentor App Premium
streams.body: Extended reports, extra tasks, personalisation and modes supporting the parent or teacher.

streams.title: Game Content & Cosmetics
streams.body: Cosmetics, skins and world aesthetic expansions — no pay-to-win, no buying knowledge.

streams.title: Schools & Institutions
streams.body: Packages for schools, classrooms, teachers, workshops and educational partnerships.

streams.title: Merchandise & Board Game
streams.body: Board games, family products, collectible world elements and offline educational materials.

roadmap.title: A plan for a world that <gold>grows.</gold>

status.title: What do we have today?
items: Book — vol. 1 ready, concepts for vols. 2 & 3
App backend & game server — alpha version

cta.title: Let's talk about the future of <gold>education.</gold>
cta.subtitle: We are looking for partners who understand that the future of education does not mean more screens — it can mean better context, stronger narrative and a thoughtfully designed growth system.
cta.info3: Eduweer — built with scale in mind

meta.title: Privacy Policy — Eduweer
meta.description: Learn how Eduweer processes your personal data in accordance with GDPR.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This privacy policy describes how Eduweer collects, uses and protects your personal data. We care about your privacy and process data only to the extent necessary to operate our services.

sections.title: 1. Data Controller
sections.body: The data controller is Radoslaw Kamysz, running the Eduweer project. Contact: hello@support.eduweer.com

sections.title: 2. Data We Collect
sections.body:
We only collect data that you voluntarily provide:
• Email address — when subscribing to the newsletter
• Name, email address, message content — when submitting the contact form

We do not collect location data, do not profile users, and do not use behavioural advertising.

sections.title: 3. Purpose and Legal Basis
sections.body:
We process your data for the following purposes:
• Newsletter — sending Eduweer updates (legal basis: consent, Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR)
• Contact form — responding to your enquiry (legal basis: legitimate interests, Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR)

sections.title: 4. Data Processors
sections.body:
We use the following sub-processors:
• Resend (resend.com) — email sending platform and newsletter list management
• Vercel (vercel.com) — application hosting and infrastructure

All sub-processors provide an adequate level of data protection under GDPR.

sections.title: 5. Data Retention
sections.body:
• Newsletter data — until consent is withdrawn (unsubscribed)
• Contact form data — for the period necessary to handle correspondence, max. 2 years

After these periods, data is permanently deleted.

sections.title: 6. Your Rights
sections.body:
You have the following rights:
• Access — ask what data we hold about you
• Rectification — correct inaccurate data
• Erasure — request deletion of your data
• Restriction — limit how we process your data
• Portability — receive your data in a structured format
• Objection — object to processing based on legitimate interests
• Withdrawal of consent — unsubscribe from the newsletter at any time

To exercise these rights, write to hello@support.eduweer.com. We will respond within 30 days.

sections.title: 7. Unsubscribing
sections.body: You can unsubscribe from the newsletter at any time by clicking the Unsubscribe link in any email from us, or by contacting us: hello@support.eduweer.com

sections.title: 8. Cookies
sections.body: This site does not use cookies for analytics or advertising. We only use technical session cookies necessary for the site to function.

sections.title: 9. Data Security
sections.body: We apply appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect your data. Data transmission occurs over an encrypted HTTPS connection.

sections.title: 10. Policy Changes
sections.body: We reserve the right to update this policy. Newsletter subscribers will be notified of significant changes. The current version is always available at eduweer.com/privacy.

sections.title: 11. Supervisory Authority
sections.body: If you believe we are processing your data unlawfully, you have the right to lodge a complaint with your local data protection authority. In Poland: UODO, ul. Stawki 2, 00-193 Warsaw, www.uodo.gov.pl

meta.title: Eduweer Trial Artifacts — Workbooks for Children
meta.description: Discover six Eduweer workbooks and collect Trial Artifacts. Activities support handwriting readiness, focus, logic, creativity, emotions, and boundaries.

hero.title: Collect the Six Trial Artifacts
hero.subtitle: Each workbook is a separate adventure guided by one of the Eduweer Guardians. Your child develops important skills, and after completing the workbook, they can unlock an artifact and assign it to their hero.

how.title: How do artifacts work in Eduweer?
how.description: Completing a workbook does not end the adventure on paper. A parent can scan the QR code, assign the unlocked artifact to a child profile, and track the hero’s progress in the Eduweer parent panel.

steps.title: Choose a workbook
steps.description: Each workbook trains a different skill and is guided by a different Guardian.

steps.title: Complete the trial
steps.description: The child works through the exercises, develops specific skills, and discovers the story behind the artifact.

steps.title: Scan the QR code
steps.description: After the workbook is completed, the parent scans the code and chooses which child should receive the reward.

steps.title: Unlock the artifact
steps.description: The artifact is added to the hero profile and becomes part of the child’s larger development path.

parentBenefits.title: Artifacts come alive in the parent panel
parentBenefits.description: After completing a workbook, you can assign the artifact to your child’s account, track completed trials, and build their hero path in the world of Eduweer.

features.title: Assign artifacts
features.description: Scan the QR code from the workbook and add the unlocked artifact to your child’s profile.

features.title: Track the collection
features.description: See which trials have been completed and which artifacts your child has already earned.

features.title: Add multiple children
features.description: Manage the progress of more than one child from a single parent account.

features.title: Get ready for more adventures
features.description: The parent account will connect workbooks, books, and future Eduweer activities.

world.title: Part of the larger Eduweer world
world.description: Trial Artifacts are part of the larger Eduweer world. Children meet characters through stories, practice concrete skills in workbooks, and parents can track progress and hero development in the parent panel.

items.title: Books
items.description: Discover the story, lands, and heroes of Eduweer.

items.title: Workbooks
items.description: Complete Guardian trials and develop concrete skills.

items.title: Parent panel
items.description: Assign artifacts, track progress, and manage your child’s account.

items.title: Hero adventure
items.description: Collect artifacts and build the young hero’s development path.

items.q: Do I need a parent account to buy a workbook?
items.a: No. You can buy a workbook independently. A parent account is needed when you want to assign the unlocked artifact to your child’s profile and track hero progress.

items.q: How do I assign an artifact to a child?
items.a: After completing the workbook, the parent scans the QR code, logs in, and chooses the child who should receive the artifact.

items.q: Can the same artifact be unlocked more than once?
items.a: The same code and artifact should be assigned only once to the selected child. This keeps the collection organized and fair.

items.q: What age are the workbooks for?
items.a: The workbooks are mainly designed for early primary school children, but the difficulty may vary depending on the trial and the child’s individual skills.

items.q: Are the workbooks part of a larger series?
items.a: Yes. The workbooks, books, parent panel, and future Eduweer activities form one connected world of development and adventure.

items.q: Do the exercises replace therapy or diagnosis?
items.a: No. The workbooks are educational and developmental. They may support focus, emotions, graphomotor skills, and self-regulation, but they do not replace professional consultation.

finalCta.title: Start the first trial
finalCta.description: Choose a workbook, create a parent account, and help your child collect new artifacts in the world of Eduweer.

auralis.title: Auralis and the Amulet of the First Rune
auralis.description: Auralis guides young heroes through the world of lines, symbols, and first letters. This workbook helps children practice a steady hand, focus, and early writing readiness. Each page is a small trial of patience — because even the greatest magic begins with one carefully drawn line.
auralis.parentDescription: This workbook supports handwriting readiness, line control, and focused practice without pressure.
inside: a short trial of the Rune Guardian
auralis.quote: Not every magic begins with a flash. Sometimes it begins with one well-drawn line.

narin.title: Narin and the Artifact of the Silent Trail
narin.description: Narin teaches patience, orientation, and careful line control. In his trial, children follow mazes, paths, and hidden passages while practicing movement planning and hand control.
narin.parentDescription: This workbook supports spatial orientation, patience, planning, and controlled hand movement within clear boundaries.
narin.quote: The path usually exists. You just have to stop looking so loudly.

tivia.title: Tivia and the Artifact of the Amber Gear
tivia.description: Tivia invites children into the world of shapes, patterns, and clever puzzles. In her workbook, simple forms turn into pictures, missing parts return to their places, and logic tasks show that every problem can be solved step by step.
tivia.parentDescription: This workbook supports logical thinking, visual analysis, pattern recognition, and patient problem solving.
tivia.quote: If something does not work, it does not mean it is broken. Maybe you simply have not discovered how it wants to work.

liora.title: Liora and the Artifact of the Green Vine
liora.description: Liora leads children through the world of nature, colors, and calm creation. This workbook is filled with plants, animals, magical gardens, and coloring pages. It is a trial of patience, harmony, and careful observation.
liora.parentDescription: This workbook helps children calm down, develop creativity, and practice completing work patiently.

maela.title: Maela and the Artifact of the Warm Shield
maela.description: Maela teaches that heroism is not only courage, but also care, calmness, and noticing others. In her workbook, children recognize emotions, help characters find safe places, and practice simple calming strategies.
maela.parentDescription: This workbook supports emotion recognition, talking about needs, self-regulation, and building a sense of safety. It may be especially helpful for children who get frustrated quickly, have a lot of energy, or benefit from simple calming routines.
maela.quote: Not every hero stands at the front of the journey. Some walk last so no one is left alone.

boran.title: Boran and the Artifact of the Red Ribbon
boran.description: Boran shows that true strength is not about pushing, shouting, or hitting. A true warrior can stop, protect the weaker, and use courage wisely. This workbook helps children practice movement control, patience, boundaries, and responsibility for their own strength.
boran.parentDescription: This workbook supports children in understanding boundaries, controlling impulses, and choosing safe reactions. It helps start conversations about the fact that being bigger, stronger, or faster does not give anyone the right to hurt others.
inside: short scenes about strength and responsibility
boran.quote: Courage without control is only noise. A true warrior knows when to move and when to stop.
